subtitle: Awards deadline imminent; team absences create meeting gaps
context: Michelle Williams sent an automatic reply on 29 June indicating she is absent, blocking progress on the Staff EES Information for HESA - Research Data Items meeting scheduled for 1 July at 11:00 with Nathan Kirwan. Asta Palmer is on compassionate leave today (30 June) and you confirmed this to Marie Cooksey on 26 June. The Awards for Excellence Scheme deadline is 3 July 2026 - you nominated Michael O'Sullivan on 29 June and need Marie Cooksey and Simon Burford to review it. An unread email from Jasmin Khokhra (26 June, 48+ hours old) confirms the UCEA Clinical pay uplift for April 2026 was completed by Tracy before leave. James Salas Guillen sent multiple unread emails about the Health Surveillance Review (24 June) and the Cority Product User Group minutes (24 June), both marked priority 1. Case 68974493 from Julie Hickman was actioned by you on 30 June morning and you confirmed it should be live. Open first: the Jasmin Khokhra email about UCEA Clinical pay uplift to understand what closure actions remain.

prioritiesToday.text: WFM / GLAM Rostering -- set up resolution meeting
prioritiesToday.description: GLAM decided at the last minute they did not have capacity to load rostering data into WFM. One wave was loaded, one was not. Three areas are now outstanding: SBS, PEDS, GLAM. This is blocking Sarah from running UC dashboards and reports. Handover notes from Michael were shared on 23 June. A separate resolution meeting is needed to establish what wasn't loaded and why, agree an approach, and produce an action plan. Meeting attendees: Kevin, Simon, Marie, Michelle, and potentially Julie given her proximity to the project. Note: WFM adoption is split -- staff who never used TeamSeer are positive; those who did are making direct comparisons, but TeamSeer was never calculating correctly. The recommendation is to bring vocal blockers like Steph Sparks into future system procurement from the start.
actions: [23 Jun 2026] Michael shared handover notes on WFM rostering status.
[24 Jun 2026] Raised at HR Systems Managers Meeting -- three areas outstanding (SBS, PEDS, GLAM). GLAM did not load rostering data at last minute. Blocking Sarah's UC dashboards and reports.
[TODO] Set up resolution meeting -- Kevin, Simon, Marie, Michelle, and potentially Julie. Agenda: understand what wasn't loaded and why, agree approach, produce action list, address GLAM rostering.
prioritiesToday.ai_summary: Three areas (SBS, PEDS, GLAM) blocking Sarah's UC dashboards after GLAM failed to load rostering data. Next: set up resolution meeting with Kevin, Simon, Marie, Michelle, and potentially Julie to understand what wasn't loaded, agree approach, and produce action list.

prioritiesToday.description: Helen Harbour sent two emails in the week of 17-18 June related to DTP1334: a SHSMS Evaluation Briefing placeholder document and a Strategic H&S Modular System requirements evaluation weightings spreadsheet (Sonhal Mistry also involved). Both are directly relevant to the POC work Kevin needs to do with Christopher Sanders (t022).
actions: [17 Jun 2026] Helen Harbour sent DTP1334 SHSMS Evaluation Briefing placeholder -- RED, act today (now overdue).
[18 Jun 2026] Helen Harbour sent Strategic H&S Modular System requirements evaluation weightings (Sonhal Mistry also on thread).
[TODO] Review Helen's evaluation briefing document.
[TODO] Review the evaluation weightings spreadsheet.
[TODO] Use both to inform POC meeting notes before sending to Chris (t022).
prioritiesToday.ai_summary: Helen Harbour's DTP1334 SHSMS Evaluation Briefing and weightings spreadsheet received 17-18 Jun (now overdue RED item). Next: review both documents and use to inform POC meeting notes before sending to Chris.

Kevin is named in the emails and a response is expected. Inbox AI triage incorrectly classified as FYI based on subject line alone without reading the body.

This leaves no first-line HR Systems support cover for that week. Kevin needs to decide who covers, what can be deferred, and whether Simon Burford or Marie Cooksey need to be informed or involved.
actions: [23 Jun 2026] Julie Hickman confirmed Michael AND Emma both absent w/c 6 July. No cover currently in place.

prioritiesToday.text: Sickness Absence Reporting -- open absence bug
prioritiesToday.description: The existing sickness absence report is not picking up open-ended (unclosed) absences. This occurs when staff return to work but the absence record in PeopleXD is not closed. As a result, individuals have gone into half pay. Issue flagged by Simon Burford at the HR Systems Managers Meeting on 24 June. A task number was shared in the meeting. Kevin to investigate whether this is a report fix (SQL/RDL) or a system data quality issue requiring a data correction process.
actions: [24 Jun 2026] Issue raised at HR Systems Managers Meeting by Simon Burford -- sickness absence report not picking up open-ended absences. Pay impact confirmed: individuals gone into half pay. Task number shared in meeting.
[TODO] Retrieve and log the task number shared at the meeting.
[TODO] Investigate root cause -- determine whether this is a report fix (SQL/RDL) or a system data issue in PeopleXD.
[TODO] Confirm fix approach and communicate resolution to Simon Burford.
prioritiesToday.ai_summary: Sickness absence report not picking up open-ended absences, causing staff to go into half pay; issue raised 24 Jun by Simon Burford. Next: retrieve task number from meeting notes, investigate root cause (report fix vs PeopleXD data issue), confirm fix approach and communicate to Simon.

prioritiesToday.text: Volunteering Hours -- forward Matt Thomas thread to Marie
prioritiesToday.description: Matt Thomas emailed Marie on 10 June and again on 19 June about the inability for staff to log their own volunteering hours in PeopleXD. This was raised during the core pilot and caused frustration and administrative burden for line managers. Marie had already met with Matt before going on leave and explained that self-logging is not currently possible in the system; he appeared satisfied at the time. He has emailed again. Action: Kevin to forward the email thread to Marie; Marie to respond directly reiterating that self-logging of volunteering hours is not possible in the current system setup. Potential alternatives noted in the meeting but not committed: a separate pay code for volunteering leave, or exposing leave items for self-service as with annual leave.
actions: [10 Jun 2026] Matt Thomas first emailed Marie about inability to self-log volunteering hours in PeopleXD.
[19 Jun 2026] Matt Thomas emailed again on the same issue.
[24 Jun 2026] Raised at HR Systems Managers Meeting -- Marie to respond reiterating that self-logging is not possible. Kevin to forward the thread to enable Marie's response.
[TODO] Kevin to forward Matt Thomas email thread to Marie.
[AWAITING] Marie to respond to Matt Thomas directly.
prioritiesToday.ai_summary: Matt Thomas raised inability to self-log volunteering hours in PeopleXD on 10 and 19 Jun; Marie to respond reiterating it's not possible. Next: forward Matt's email thread to Marie to enable her response.

prioritiesToday.ai_summary: Maura McGlynn sent catch-up email on case 69001655 with potential Devs call to arrange. Next: review full email content and assess next steps for escalated case in progress with development team.

Draft load file sent to Conor 17 Jun. 40 Pay Administered By codes still need setting up in UOXU.

prioritiesWeek.description: Smart notes in Cority CoreEHS+ (GX2) do not allow edits after save and close. Raised November 2025, dev ref CCC-19664. Cority's official position (confirmed 08 Jun 2026 by Mehran Riaz, support leadership): this is an INTENDED feature -- not a bug or missing feature. Cority classify the request to allow post-save edits as an enhancement, and it is NOT on their current roadmap. No timeline will be provided. Beta/early access ('enhanced access support') requires Premium Support, which Oxford does not have. Oxford has no named account manager -- we are in a pool.
actions: [Nov 2025] Ticket 00476764 raised by James -- smart notes cannot be edited after save/close.

[08 Jun 2026] Mehran Riaz (support leadership) responded: feature is INTENDED behaviour, classified as enhancement, NOT on roadmap. No timeline. Premium Support required for beta access (Oxford does not have it). No named account manager -- pool model.
[16 Jun 2026] Raised at Cority PUG with Christopher Sanders. PUG acknowledged smart notes as outstanding; Kevin to escalate directly with Cority for a firm delivery date.
[TODO] Chase Cority for firm delivery date on CCC-19664 draft/save functionality.
[TODO] Decide whether to pursue via sales/account route or accept as a known limitation and communicate to stakeholders.
prioritiesWeek.ai_summary: Cority smart notes cannot be edited after save/close (ticket 00476764, dev ref CCC-19664); Cority confirmed 8 Jun this is INTENDED behaviour, not on roadmap. Kevin escalated at Cority PUG 16 Jun; no premium support or named account manager. Next: chase Cority for firm delivery date on CCC-19664 or decide whether to pursue via sales/account route.

prioritiesWeek.text: Web pages and URL linking -- job posting updates
prioritiesWeek.description: Internal Mobility Principles page moved to a new OxIntranet URL. Three pages needed link updates: main ops intranet page, professional services search page, and individual vacancy application pages. New URL confirmed live on 3 June. Old page retired 23/24 June. E-Form save bug in Search Appointments V4 (case 69001655) was blocking updates -- Maura McGlynn (AG) applied a config fix on 23 June. Kevin was able to update the Login screen on UOXU on 25 June, but was unable to update Current Job Vacancies and Job Search screens. Devs are now handling the remaining UOXU screens; Kevin to validate before going live. Case 69001655 is the related Access Group escalation case for this work.
actions: [03 Jun 2026] Meeting with Laura Porter -- reviewed three pages needing link updates.
[19 Jun 2026] Kevin escalated case 69001655 to Sarah Murphy (Access Group) for assistance with the URL linking work. (email: Kevin (sent to: Sarah Murphy) - Escalation Request – Case 69001655)

[23 Jun 2026] Urgent escalation -- unable to save changes in Search Appointments V4 E-Form Maintenance (case 69001655). Maura McGlynn (AG) applied a config fix -- reports changes saving in Production, OXU, and OXZ.

[25 Jun 2026] Kevin updated case 69001655 with Dev team information and asked Sarah Murphy to progress further. (email: Kevin (sent to: Sarah Murphy) - Re: #External# Escalation Request – Case 69001655)
[25 Jun 2026] Kevin updated Login screen on UOXU successfully -- 'Internal Mobility Principles' hyperlinked, spacing added after 'Existing University Staff' and 'New Applicants:' headings.
[25 Jun 2026] Unable to update Current Job Vacancies and Job Search screens on UOXU -- devs now handling remaining UOXU screens.
[AWAITING] Maura McGlynn (AG) to send Teams message when remaining UOXU screens are ready for Kevin to validate before going LIVE.

[30 Jun 2026] Kevin confirmed to Laura Porter that IM Principles URL changes have been made and verified on test environment. (email: Kevin (sent to: Laura Porter) - Re: IM Principles - change to URL)
prioritiesWeek.ai_summary: Internal Mobility Principles URL migration completed: Login screen updated 25 Jun, but Current Job Vacancies and Job Search screens on UOXU still pending dev team. Next: await Maura McGlynn Teams message when remaining screens ready for Kevin to validate before going LIVE.

Users without university emails -- James investigating with supplier.

prioritiesWeek.text: Holiday Records - 3 Reports Created -- approved, awaiting scheduling (case 69001638)
prioritiesWeek.description: Access Group Project Scope Document (case 69001638) received and approved 24 June 2026. Scope: 3 Holiday Records reports built by Payroll Consultant (2.5 days, 1,750pts) and WFM Consultant (1.5 days, 1,050pts). Total 2,800 flex points. Reports: Annual Leave Record by Person and Leave Year; Holiday Pay and Accrual Record for Variable-Hours and Casual Arrangements; Holiday Pay in Lieu on Termination. Access Group T&C: consultant will make contact within 3 weeks of approval to agree delivery timelines. 3-month grace period applies to flex point expiry (29 Jun expiry = use by ~29 Sep 2026). Asta Palmer to be involved in delivery.
actions: [15 Jun 2026] Kevin initiated quote request with Access Group (Mike West).
[20 Jun 2026] Ann-Marie Topliss confirmed case 69001638 transitioned to Optimisation Services.
[24 Jun 2026] Quote received (Alan Quirke, V1.0, dated 12 Jun). 2,800 flex points total.
[24 Jun 2026] Flex points approved by Kevin for case 69001638. Awaiting Access Group consultant to reach out and agree delivery timelines (within 3 weeks of approval per T&C).

[24 Jun 2026] Raised at HR Systems Managers Meeting. Access Group noted as notoriously slow. Statutory requirement means timeline cannot slip indefinitely. Third week of August: Marie Kay away, statutory returns season. Agreement: Kevin to reach out to team to agree delivery timing collectively, factoring in HEAT, July leave, and ~29 Sep flex point expiry.

prioritiesWeek.ai_summary: Access Group Project (case 69001638) approved 24 Jun: 3 Holiday Records reports (2.5 + 1.5 days, 2,800 flex points total). Deadline ~29 Sep flex point expiry. Next: reach out to team to agree delivery timing collectively factoring HEAT, July leave, and statutory returns season; confirm Asta Palmer involvement and Marie Cooksey sign-off.

prioritiesWeek.text: REF attributes via ESS -- end of July deadline (Sarah Rowles)
prioritiesWeek.description: Sarah Rowles has added a new item to the HR Systems Roadmap: sharing specific REF attributes with staff via Employee Self-Service (ESS) by end of July 2026, to enable the REF appeals process. Simon Burford and Kevin Lelitte are named. Nathan (Sarah's team) is looking at it from the technical side. Anne is sharing last time's guidance, but Sarah has flagged this is NOT a like-for-like repeat. Key question in thread: will this require a new UDF to be set up and data loaded, or re-use an existing UDF? Athena Artuso noted that UDFs were set up in advance for future REF cycles, so that stage may be future-proof -- but it depends on what the specific REF attributes are. Sarah has explicitly flagged that the team is thin on the ground in July and this will need FA input, testing time, and business change support for user instructions.
actions: [24 Jun 2026] Sarah Rowles flagged new Roadmap item -- REF attributes via ESS, end of July deadline, for REF appeals process.

prioritiesWeek.text: DTP1334 SHSMS Evaluation -- ongoing post-meeting (resourcing, scoring, key dates)
prioritiesWeek.description: H&S Management System (SHSMS) evaluation (DTP1334) is underway. Implementation expected to start 20 July -- 4 weeks from meeting date. Kevin's position: HR Systems needs dedicated project resource for this, not absorbed into BAU. Scoring methodology agreed: Requirements 65%, Price 30%, References 5%. Christopher Sanders is Kevin's H&S-side counterpart on the evaluation. Key dates: follow-up required by 2 July; evaluator briefings 14 July; supplier responses approximately 21 July.

prioritiesWeek.text: HR Reporting SSO Migration -- Managed Desktop (Roadmap 179)
prioritiesWeek.description: Migration of HRIS HR Reporting and Tableau users from CONNECT accounts to SSO on the Oxford Managed Desktop. Roadmap item 179. Lead: Kevin, Team: Asta. Project started November 2025 following initial call with IT Services (John Jones, Richard Jessett, Sadiq Abbasi, Desktop Services). Trial user list agreed February 2026: Simon Burford ADMN2716, David Sanders UOHS0054, Asta Palmer OUIT0422, Kevin Lelitte BEGB0037. Henry Acheampong ADMN5896 excluded at Kevin's request. Kevin migrated successfully March 2026 -- no issues. Stuart (Desktop Services) instructed to proceed with remaining users excluding Henry. Project stalled since March 2026; April 2026 deadline passed. Technical blocker identified at Managers Meeting 24 June: Visual Studio 2022 resolves the compatibility issue but is currently on a trial licence only. Do not migrate further users until VS2022 licensing is confirmed. Contacts: Desktop Services dstprojects@it.ox.ac.uk, Sadiq Abbasi, John Jones, Richard Jessett.

prioritiesWeek.text: Work Inbox -- render CC Today/This Week tasks as cards in priority panel
prioritiesWeek.description: Work Inbox and Command Centre work in tandem. Currently the CC button appears on work-inbox priority cards only when an email has been linked to a CC task by Phase 3.6. The gap: CC Today/This Week tasks that have no current email (e.g. t015 -- originating email is 22 days old, outside the 50-email fetch window) never appear in work-inbox at all. Kevin's workflow: scan Work Inbox first for morning overview, click CC button to go straight into the task. This should work for all active CC tasks, not just ones with a recent email. Card design: two visible tags on the card face -- Email tag (clickable, opens email via openmail:// using entryId, only shown if entryId is set) and CC tag (links to Command Centre task). The email open tag is more naturally at home in Work Inbox than in CC since WI is email-first. Cards visually distinct from inbox email cards -- CC-sourced label so Kevin knows at a glance where they came from. Exclude done tasks and Parked tier.
actions: [25 Jun 2026] Feature scoped by Kevin -- CC Today/This Week tasks should appear in Work Inbox priority panel with CC button. Originating issue: t015 not visible in work-inbox because its email is 22 days old and outside the 50-email fetch window.

[25 Jun 2026] Card design refined -- two tags on card face: Email tag (openmail:// via entryId, shown only if entryId set) and CC tag (links to Command Centre). Email open is more relevant in WI than CC since WI is email-first; both tags should appear in both dashboards but Email tag is the primary action in WI.
[TODO] Implement in work-inbox index.html: fetch tasks.json from CC repo on load (cache-busted).
[TODO] Render Today-tier CC tasks in work-inbox Today priority section; This Week-tier in This Week section.
[TODO] Each card: title, status field, Email tag (openmail:// if entryId set), CC tag (Command Centre link).
[TODO] Exclude done tasks (commandCentre_done_v1 localStorage) and Parked tier.
[TODO] Visual distinction from inbox email cards -- CC-sourced label.
[TODO] Screenshot and get Kevin approval before pushing to main.
prioritiesWeek.ai_summary: Work Inbox enhancement: render CC Today/This Week tasks as cards in priority panel even when no current email linked (e.g. t015 email 22 days old, outside 50-email fetch window). Next: build rendering logic for Today/This Week tiers, add Email and CC tags, exclude done and Parked tasks, get Kevin approval before pushing to main.

prioritiesWeek.text: PACS org structure -- college entities L2 to L3 impact assessment
prioritiesWeek.description: Katherine Corr (PACS team) is proposing to adjust how Oxford Colleges are recorded in the org structure -- moving them from L2 to L3 (with an appropriate L2 parent) so they can be used in PXD to record researcher personal data for REF and in the new DARS system. Currently Colleges, Societies and Halls are all set up at L2. Simon Burford asked Kevin on 29 Jun to work with James and Chris to establish the impact from a PeopleXD / H&S Systems point of view, then report back so the management team can pull together a formal response. Martin McGovern (ORMS) and Robert Bradley (IAM) have both confirmed the change would be fine for their respective systems.
actions: [29 Jun 2026] Simon Burford asked Kevin to assess the impact with James and Chris. Kevin confirmed he would pick it up.
[TODO] Work with James and Chris to assess impact on PeopleXD and H&S systems of moving Colleges from L2 to L3 in org structure.
[TODO] Report back to Simon so the management team can pull together a formal response to Katherine Corr (PACS).

Simon Burford asked Kevin 29 Jun to assess impact with James and Chris.
